key_insight: SamatNext v0.2-B appears to improve curriculum retention by balancing plasticity and forgetting through a hybrid decoder design with RMS normalization and scale calibration.
engineering_relevance: For sequential model adaptation in code and scientific workflows, the result suggests hybrid state-space/attention decoders may retain later-task performance better than standard Transformers under staged fine-tuning.
method: 356M-parameter hybrid decoder alternating Differential-Attention-style layers and DeltaNet-inspired linear-state mixer layers
Uses RMS normalization plus output scale calibration
Evaluated on a controlled staged Python code curriculum against a parameter-matched Transformer baseline
evidence_signal: On the controlled Stage 5 holdout, SamatNext v0.2-B reached 100.0% pass rate and retained 98.8% of Stage 3 behavior, compared with 97.6% and 6.0% for the strongest Transformer baseline.
watch_item: The study is narrowly controlled and still shows weak long-horizon early-stage retention, so the retention gains should not be treated as a general cure for catastrophic forgetting.
paper_title: SamatNext v0.2-B: An Exploratory Study of RMS-Normalized Hybrid Decoders for Curriculum Retention in Small Code Models
